_note: All rated quantities the designer must specify on the assembly schedule. Standard-preferred values per IEC 61439-1 Clause 5.

Ue.name: Rated operational voltage
Ue.definition: Voltage between phases (a.c.) or between poles (d.c.) at which the assembly is designed to operate continuously.

Ui.name: Rated insulation voltage
Ui.definition: Voltage to which the assembly's dielectric strength is referred. Must be ≥ Ue.

Uimp.name: Rated impulse withstand voltage
Uimp.definition: Peak value of an impulse voltage that the assembly's insulation can withstand under specified conditions. Selected based on overvoltage category.

overvoltage_category_to_Uimp_for_400V.IV: 6 kV (origin of installation — meter, main switchgear)

In.name: Rated current of the assembly
In.definition: Maximum sum of currents from incoming circuits, declared by the manufacturer. Used with RDF for verification.

Inc.name: Rated current of a circuit of the assembly
Inc.definition: Maximum continuous current that an individual outgoing circuit can carry.

Icw.name: Rated short-time withstand current
Icw.definition: RMS short-circuit current the assembly can carry for a stated duration. See short-circuit-withstand.json for full treatment.

Ipk.name: Rated peak withstand current
Ipk.definition: Peak instantaneous current the assembly must withstand. Ratio Ipk/Icw via n-factor.

Icc.name: Rated conditional short-circuit current
Icc.definition: Prospective fault current when withstand relies on an upstream current-limiting device.

fn.name: Rated frequency
fn.definition: Frequency at which the assembly is designed to operate.
fn.typical: 50 Hz in Europe, Africa, most of Asia; 60 Hz in North/South America and parts of Asia

pollution_degree.name: Pollution degree
pollution_degree.definition: Severity of environmental contamination at clearances and creepages.

values.3: Industrial — conductive pollution may be expected (typical industrial floor)
values.4: Continuous conductive pollution by dust, rain — outdoor

overvoltage_category.name: Overvoltage category
overvoltage_category.definition: Determines required Uimp based on the assembly's position in the installation.

values.I: Equipment connected to circuits with overvoltage control (signal-only, separated by SPDs at every interface)
values.II: Appliances and final circuits — fed from fixed installation
values.III: Distribution circuits — within the installation (DBs, mains)
values.IV: Origin of the installation — meter, main intake, main switchgear, things upstream of the consumer unit

overvoltage_category.typical_LV_building: IV at supply intake / MSB; III at sub-DB; II at final circuits
